title: Aspidium preslianum
intro:

Aspidium ferns are a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. These ferns have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing and immune system regulation. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium ferns include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to their medicinal properties. Aspidium ferns are commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Aspidium preslianum belongs to the family Aspleniaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the maidenhair spleenworts.
